skills.pageDesc: Manage skills available to Claude Code. Skills marked as auto-inject will be enabled automatically when creating new workspaces

mcpServices.pageDesc: Manage MCP services auto-injected into new workspaces. These services are automatically added when creating workspaces.

toolbar.jsonViewHint: This view is read-only. It shows the current auto-inject MCP services as JSON, which you can copy to use with Claude Desktop or other tools.
toolbar.autoInjectTooltip: When enabled, this MCP service will be automatically added to new workspaces

import.title: Import MCP Services from JSON
import.hint: Paste Claude Desktop format MCP config JSON (JSONC comments supported). Servers will be parsed and added to the auto-inject list. Existing servers with the same name will be skipped.
import.parseError: Failed to parse JSON, please check the format
import.formatError: Invalid format. Expected { mcpServers: { ... } } or { name: { ... } }
import.allLocalFileError: All STDIO services reference local file paths and cannot be imported. Only package manager commands (npx/uvx) are supported.

empty.title: No auto-inject MCP services
empty.hint: Click "Add Manually" or "From Template" to add the first auto-inject service

notifications.addSuccess: MCP service added to auto-inject list
notifications.deleteSuccess: MCP service removed from auto-inject list

deleteDialog.title: Remove Auto-Inject Service
deleteDialog.content: Remove "{{name}}" from the auto-inject list? Existing workspace configurations will not be affected.

resetDialog.title: ⚠ Reset Memory
resetDialog.warning: This will permanently delete all memory files for this workspace and cannot be recovered!
resetDialog.content: After clearing memory, the CC sandbox will lose all learned user preferences, task records, and knowledge. The workspace will start fresh.
resetDialog.hint: Recommended when: workspace behavior is abnormal, or you need to fully reset the CC sandbox.

ccDesignDialog.title: Let CC Design a New Skill
ccDesignDialog.info:
Clicking confirm will navigate to the Comm tab and pre-fill a design prompt.
You can add your specific requirements in the input box before sending.
ccDesignDialog.desc: CC will use the skill-creator guide to save the designed skill in the dynamic skills directory. You can view, edit, or promote it anytime from the Extensions tab.

deleteDynamicDialog.title: Confirm Delete
deleteDynamicDialog.content: Are you sure you want to delete dynamic skill {{name}}? This action cannot be recovered.

mcpTemplateDialog.title: Fill MCP Config Template
mcpTemplateDialog.warning: This will overwrite the existing MCP config. Please confirm you have a backup or don't need to keep the current config.
mcpTemplateDialog.desc: The template includes JSONC examples for common MCP services (Filesystem, GitHub, Brave Search, Fetch, Memory, PostgreSQL), all commented out by default. Uncomment and fill in parameters as needed.

ccRules.title: Execution Rules
ccRules.description: Manage long-lived rules for both CC and NA.
ccRules.subtitle: CC / NA configured separately
ccRules.tooltip: This section contains two layers: execution rules for CC and wording / communication rules visible only to NA.

sharedFacts.title: Fixed Facts
sharedFacts.description: Stable facts shared by both sides.

prompt.systemPromptHint: Expand to view the current content
prompt.naContextHint: Current status, latest progress, temporary notes
prompt.naContextDesc: Use this for time-sensitive information. Later tasks may continue updating it.
prompt.naContextPlaceholder:
For example:
- The current main task is reorganizing workspace guidance
- A new entry path and key file locations have been confirmed
- A file for explanation is already available in the shared directory
prompt.sharedFactsHint: Shared by both sides and kept stable
prompt.sharedFactsDesc: Use this for long-lived facts and constraints. Put anything here that should not be changed automatically.
prompt.sharedFactsPlaceholder:
For example:
- Frontend checks must be completed before submission
- Direct pushes to the main branch are forbidden
- Page copy should stay clear and avoid too many internal terms
prompt.ccRulesDesc: Use this for coding style, delivery preferences, and output format requirements.
prompt.extraPlaceholder:
Enter custom instructions here, e.g.:

## Personal Preferences

- Use English for all code comments
- Prefer Python for problem solving
prompt.naRulesDesc: Use this for wording style, communication boundaries, and response preferences.
prompt.naRulesPlaceholder:
For example:
- Start with the conclusion before going into detail
- When mentioning the collaboration summary, remind the user that it may continue to be updated later
editorGuideItems: If you are correcting what the workspace is like right now, write it in the collaboration state summary.
If you are declaring a stable fact or hard constraint that should continue to hold, write it in shared fixed facts.
If you only want to constrain CC behavior, write it in execution rules instead of mixing it into _na_context.
prompt.naContextWarning: _na_context can be updated automatically by CC. If a piece of information must not be rewritten, move it to Shared Fixed Facts or NA-only Rules.

notifications.naContextSaveSuccess: Collaboration state summary saved. Future CC delegations will be reminded to review the latest version first.
